rationale: A precision strike on Tethys’ border radar installations will degrade their situational awareness and signal resolve without triggering full escalation.
Conduct large-scale drills near the Tethys border to simulate invasion and maintain pressure while reinforcing troop readiness.
Disrupt Tethys’ military logistics networks to slow reinforcement cycles and create asymmetric battlefield advantage.
Stage a provocation in neutral waters implicating Tethys to fracture international support and justify our military posture.
Cripple key energy transit nodes in Tethys to weaken their economic leverage and force diplomatic concessions.
Leak selectively doctored Tethys military plans to key neutrals to build coalition sympathy without direct commitment.
Secure reserves of critical energy and food imports now to insulate against Tethys-led supply disruptions during the crisis.
Flood black markets with counterfeit Tethys scrip to erode confidence in their financial system and amplify sanction effects.
Offer preferential energy deals to Meridian in exchange for diplomatic non-alignment, fracturing potential opposition coalitions.
Demonstrating readiness on our border deters further Novaris escalation while staying below the threshold of war.
Disrupting command-and-control systems in Novaris creates asymmetric leverage without direct kinetic engagement.
Positioning fast-attack craft along key maritime chokepoints signals resolve and complicates enemy logistics.
Covertly degrading critical energy infrastructure nodes used by Novaris to bypass sanctions undermines their economic resilience.
Orchestrating a plausible false-flag event in a third country can fracture Novaris’s diplomatic alliances.
Exfiltrating data on Novaris’s military supply chains enables future precision targeting and strategic anticipation.
Rallying moderate powers to a joint statement strengthens our legal position and isolates Novaris diplomatically.
Delivering aid through international channels bolsters our moral authority and undermines Novaris’s narrative of regional dominance.
Exploring discreet off-ramps preserves escalation options while testing whether Novaris seeks de-escalation.
Securing reserves of food, fuel, and medical supplies insulates us from short-term shocks and increases bargaining endurance.
Imposing targeted export bans on dual-use technologies amplifies pressure on Novaris’s military-industrial base.
Coordinating with allies to weaken Novaris’s currency erodes their import capacity and fuels domestic discontent.
